key_insight: A multimodal pre-treatment signature combining clinical, immune, soluble, pathology, transcriptomic, and genomic features predicted primary resistance to anti-PD-(L)1 therapy better than any single biomarker in advanced NSCLC.
clinical_relevance: This approach may help identify patients unlikely to benefit from anti-PD-(L)1 therapy before treatment starts, supporting more personalized first-line and later-line treatment decisions.
method: Prospective multicentre study in 439 advanced NSCLC patients across 17 centers.
Pre-treatment profiling across six layers: clinical data, routine labs, circulating immune phenotyping, soluble vascular markers, digital pathology, transcriptomics, and genomics.
Benchmarked 36 feature-selection methods and 10 machine-learning models with .632 optimism correction.
evidence_signal: The final multimodal model achieved an optimism-corrected AUROC of 0.73 ± 0.05 and PPV of 0.63 ± 0.07 overall, outperforming PD-L1 and TMB alone.
watch_item: The model is promising but needs external validation and practical simplification before routine clinical implementation, especially given treatment-setting-dependent features.
paper_title: Multimodal profiling for prediction of primary resistance to anti-PD-(L)1 therapy in advanced non-small-cell lung cancer: the prospective PIONeeR biomarkers study
